meaning: For that reason; used to show that the next statement is a result of the previous one.
example: The school was closed due to snow; therefore, the children stayed home.

meaning: As a result of something that happened before.
example: He didn't study for the test; consequently, he did not pass.

meaning: In this way, or as a result of what has just been mentioned.
example: She won the tournament, thus proving she was the best player.

meaning: For this reason; used to introduce a logical consequence.
example: The store was closed for repairs, hence the lack of customers.

meaning: In addition to what has already been said; used to add more support to a point.
example: The new computer is faster; furthermore, it uses less electricity.

meaning: Used to add an important new fact or argument that supports your point.
example: The house is beautiful; moreover, it is located in a very quiet neighborhood.

meaning: Used to show a clear difference between two things or situations.
example: The first half of the movie was exciting; in contrast, the second half was boring.

meaning: Used to introduce a contrasting point of view or alternative fact.
example: Working from home saves time; on the other hand, you might feel isolated.

meaning: From an opposite point of view; used to introduce an idea that is the reverse of another.
example: Poor diet leads to illness; conversely, healthy eating promotes good health.

meaning: Used to show that two situations keep following each other, or to offer a second choice.
example: We could drive to the beach, or alternately, we could take the train.

meaning: Used to introduce a statement that makes the main statement surprising or less strong.
example: Although the sun was shining, the wind was very cold.

meaning: Used to emphasize a contrast or a fact that makes something else surprising.
example: She went to work even though she was feeling quite sick.

meaning: Only if a particular condition is met; on the condition that.
example: You can attend the party, provided that you finish your homework first.

meaning: Except on the condition that; used to show the only situation that stops something.
example: You cannot enter the building unless you have a security pass.

meaning: For this reason; introducing the cause behind an outcome.
example: The team practiced daily. Because of this, they won the championship easily.

meaning: Because of; caused by a specific event or situation.
example: The flight was delayed due to thick fog at the airport.

meaning: Introducing the outcome or consequence of a previous action.
example: The company cut its prices, and as a result, sales increased.

meaning: Used to show that the opposite of what has just been said is actually true.
example: I thought you disliked vegetables. On the contrary, I eat them every day.

meaning: Used to explain something in a simpler or different way to make it clearer.
example: We don't have enough money. In other words, we cannot buy the house.

meaning: Used to clarify the exact meaning of what you have just said.
example: Our group will meet next week â€” that is to say, on Tuesday afternoon.

meaning: In a detailed or exact way; used to point out a single item or detail.
example: I am asking you, specifically, to double-check the final numbers.

meaning: For example; used to introduce a particular case that represents a general rule.
example: Wild animals can be dangerous; for instance, bears will attack if threatened.

meaning: For the same reasons; in the same way.
example: If you work hard, you will succeed; by the same token, laziness leads to failure.

meaning: Speaking about that topic; connecting your next point to the current subject.
example: It's getting late. On that note, let's end the meeting for today.

meaning: Also; as an extra point or item.
example: She plays the violin. In addition, she speaks three languages fluently.

meaning: In spite of what has just been said; however.
example: It was a very difficult task; nevertheless, they completed it on time.

meaning: Despite what has been said; yet.
example: The weather was terrible. Nonetheless, we enjoyed our walk in the park.

meaning: Because of new information; considering a particular fact.
example: In light of the new report, we have decided to change our policy.

meaning: Using something as the reason or foundation for an idea or action.
example: He was offered the job on the basis of his excellent past experience.

meaning: In a way that fits the situation; therefore.
example: The costs have risen, and we must adjust our prices accordingly.
